ontology.foodex2_description: Burrito — wheat flour tortilla filled with meat, rice, beans, and cheese
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken burrito as a composite dish; SNOMED CT coverage of prepared mixed dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large chicken burrito weighing approximately 400 g, as typically served at a fast-casual Mexican restaurant or food outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Burrito — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Micronutrients (calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for the closest analogous burrito composite and retained where plausible. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB or USDA SR Legacy are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed bean and rice burrito (GI ~50)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chicken burrito in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 50 is estimated from the closest analogous composite food: a flour tortilla-based burrito with beans and rice, consistent with published GI values for mixed bean-rice dishes (GI range 45–55, low category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 x 79.48) / 100 = 39.7 g. Carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 19.87 x 4 = 79.48 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 563 mg per 100 g; a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 2,252 mg sodium, exceeding the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive individuals should limit or avoid this dish in its standard form; if consumed, request reduced cheese, no added sauces, and low-sodium seasoning, and restrict portion to no more than 150 g to keep sodium below 850 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated GI of 50 (low) and dietary fibre 2.57 g per 100 g from beans moderate the postprandial glucose response, but a full 400 g serving delivers 79.5 g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of approximately 40, which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume a half-portion (200 g, approximately 40 g carbohydrate) alongside non-starchy vegetables; request brown rice substitution and omit sour cream to further reduce glycemic impact and saturated fat.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.0 g per 100 g and trans fat 0.13 g per 100 g from cheese and processed tortilla contribute to cardiovascular risk; sodium at 563 mg per 100 g further elevates blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 150–200 g, request reduced cheese and no sour cream to lower saturated and trans fat intake, and pair with a high-fibre side to offset the sodium and fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Contains nightshade ingredients (tomato, bell pepper, chili powder) that may trigger joint inflammation in solanine-sensitive RA patients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients sensitive to solanine should request omission of salsa, chili powder, and bell peppers; if nightshades are tolerated, the black beans and cumin provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols and fibre.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A chicken burrito is a Mexican-origin composite dish consisting of a large flour tortilla wrapped around a filling of seasoned grilled or braised chicken, steamed white rice, black or pinto beans, shredded cheese, and mixed vegetables including lettuce, tomato, and cabbage. The tortilla is typically made from refined wheat flour and is soft and pliable, providing a neutral, slightly chewy base. The filling delivers a savoury, mildly spiced flavour profile from cumin, oregano, garlic, and chili seasoning applied to the chicken. In Singapore, chicken burritos are commonly served at fast-casual Mexican-style restaurants and food delivery outlets, typically as a single large portion of approximately 350–400 g. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 193 kcal, 10.65 g protein, 7.83 g fat (3.0 g saturated), 19.87 g carbohydrate, 2.57 g dietary fibre, and 563 mg sodium. The high sodium content (563 mg per 100 g) is the primary clinical concern, making this dish unsuitable for hypertensive individuals in full-portion servings. The estimated glycemic index of 50 (low) reflects the moderating effect of beans and protein on glucose absorption, making it a more glycaemically favourable grain-based dish than plain white rice. The dish contains gluten (wheat tortilla) and dairy (cheese) and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or lactose intolerance. It is generally considered halal when prepared with halal-certified chicken and no alcohol-based ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chicken burrito?
Is chicken burrito good for diabetics?
Can I eat chicken burrito if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sodium content of a chicken burrito?
How much protein is in a chicken burrito?
What is the GI of a chicken burrito?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database at confidence 0.95. Micronutrients cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for a comparable burrito composite. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (mixed bean-rice burrito, GI ~50) with documented estimation basis. All values are internally consistent and nutritionally plausible for this dish type.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific chicken burrito composite are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for the closest analogous burrito as of 2026. Sources consulted and confirmed unavailability: HPB Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Micronutrients calcium (50 mg), iron (1.0 mg), cholesterol (22.5 mg), saturated fat (3.0 g), trans fat (0.13 g), and vitamin C (5.0 mg) were retained after cross-validation against USDA SR Legacy values for a comparable burrito and are considered plausible. The input record incorrectly tagged this dish as vegetarian and plant_based in dietary_tags — these have been corrected to false as the dish contains chicken. The input occasion_tag breakfast has been replaced with more appropriate meal contexts (lunch, dinner, takeaway). GI is an analogous estimate; a direct measurement for chicken burrito is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = 39.7 g (high GL), which is clinically significant for diabetic meal planning.
